title: Hello Search Quickstart
body: A small starter guide for teams building their first OpenSearch demo from a Solr migration sketch.
author: OSC

title: Troubleshooting Relevance Drift
body: BM25 scoring may reorder short titles compared with Solr. Start by validating field boosts and analyzer behavior.
author: Pat

title: OpenSearch Cluster Checklist
body: Verify cluster health, create the target index, and load a toy corpus before discussing production cutover.
author: Riley

title: Facet Design for Content Search
body: Categories, authors, and tags are common facet fields. Keep them keyword typed unless you need full text search.
author: Jordan

title: Migration Risks You Should Resolve First
body: Custom analyzers, nested documents, and unclear relevance goals are the three assumptions most likely to invalidate a migration plan.
author: OSC

title: Dual Write Without Drama
body: Keep the old Solr system warm, write to both engines briefly, and use shadow queries to compare real responses safely.
author: Alex

title: Simple Demo Dataset for Hello Search
body: A toy dataset should be boring on purpose. You want obvious fields, obvious queries, and no mystery behavior.
author: Morgan

title: Query Translation Examples
body: Translate Solr q and fq patterns into OpenSearch bool, multi_match, term, and range clauses before you touch application code.
author: Sam

title: Authoritative Product Decisions
body: If stakeholders really want better relevance instead of parity, say that early and rewrite the validation plan around that goal.
author: Pat

title: Date Filters and Recent Content
body: Date range filters are usually easy to port, but timezone mistakes make demos look broken fast.
author: Taylor

title: Hello Search Architecture Notes
body: The target index keeps a catch all text field so simple keyword search can hit title, body, and author together.
author: OSC

title: Operational Sanity Checks
body: Green cluster health is not enough. Confirm document counts, mappings, and a few exact queries before declaring success.
author: Riley
